text: During a robotic right hemicolectomy, the surgeon uses robotic monopolar curved scissors to score the mesentery and create a window beneath the ileocolic pedicle. The duodenum is visualized and preserved. The ileocolic pedicle is then divided using a vessel sealer for high ligation.

During a robotic right hemicolectomy, the surgeon identifies the ileocolic pedicle and creates a window beneath it by scoring the mesentery with robotic monopolar curved scissors. The duodenum is visualized and preserved, and the ileocolic pedicle is divided using a vessel sealer for high ligation.

The surgeon identifies and divides the right colic artery and vein using the vessel sealer. The right colon is mobilized to the middle colic vessels through medial to lateral dissection, developing the retro mesenteric plane. The right branch of the middle colic artery is divided with the vessel sealer. The greater omentum is dissected off the transverse colon, entering the lesser sac. The cecum, appendix, terminal ileum, and ascending colon are dissected off the right paracolic gutter. Once the right colon is fully mobilized, the robotic stapler transects the transverse colon and ileum after creating mesenteric windows with the robotic vessel sealer.

The surgeon identifies and divides the right colic artery and vein using the vessel sealer. The right colon is mobilized to the middle colic vessels through medial to lateral dissection, developing the retro mesenteric plane. The right branch of the middle colic artery is divided with the vessel sealer. The greater omentum is dissected off the transverse colon, entering the lesser sac. The cecum, appendix, terminal ileum, and ascending colon are dissected off the right paracolic gutter. Once fully mobilized, the robotic stapler transects the transverse colon and ileum after mesenteric windows are created with the robotic vessel sealer.

The fully mobilized specimen is positioned in the right lower quadrant. The surgeon creates an intracorporeal isoperistaltic ileocolonic anastomosis by making small enterotomies on the ileum and transverse colon using monopolar curved scissors. The robotic stapler is used to form the anastomosis between the ileum and transverse colon. The common defect is closed with a 15 cm 3-0 V-lock suture.

The fully mobilized specimen is placed in the right lower quadrant. The surgeon creates an intracorporeal isoperistaltic ileocolonic anastomosis by making small enterotomies on the ileum and transverse colon using monopolar curved scissors. The robotic stapler is used to complete the anastomosis between the ileum and transverse colon. The common defect is closed with a 15 cm 3-0 V-lock suture.

The specimen was extracted through a three centimeter suprapubic incision. The patient had a good recovery. He had return of bowel function on post-operative day two and was discharged to home in good condition on post-operative day four. The final pathology was a two point five centimeter cecal adenocarcinoma T two N zero with all fifty one lymph nodes being negative.
The surgeon extracts the specimen through a three-centimeter suprapubic incision.
